content.subject: Task 1.1.1: Scaffold @ax/skills package
content.description: Create package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, src/types.ts for @ax/skills. Add to workspace and root tsconfig refs. Verify build is clean. Commit.

content.subject: Task 1.1.2: Manifest parser + capability schema
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/manifest.ts with parseSkillManifest(). Schema: name kebab-case, description ≤240 chars, allowedHosts (no scheme/path/wildcard/IP), credentials with SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE slots, kind enum, inline-secret rejection at any depth, mcpServers reserved.

content.subject: Task 1.1.3: Strip capabilities from workspace SKILL.md (validator-skill)
content.description: Add stripCapabilitiesFromFrontmatter helper to packages/validator-skill/src/frontmatter.ts. Wire into workspace:pre-apply. Determine rewrite vs veto by reading core. Either strip-and-warn or veto.

content.subject: Task 1.2.1: skills_v1_skills migration
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/migrations.ts with runSkillsMigration() that creates skills_v1_skills table with columns: skill_id PRIMARY KEY, description, manifest_yaml, body_md, version, created_at, updated_at.

content.subject: Task 1.2.2: SkillsStore CRUD
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/store.ts with createSkillsStore(db) exposing list/get/upsert/delete/resolve. Capabilities re-parsed from manifest_yaml on read. resolve preserves input order, drops unknown ids.

content.subject: Task 1.2.3: @ax/skills plugin (5 service hooks)
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/plugin.ts with createSkillsPlugin() registering skills:list/get/upsert/delete/resolve service hooks. skills:delete checks agents:any-attached-to-skill structurally.

content.subject: Task 1.3.1: Admin HTTP routes for /admin/skills
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts. GET/POST /admin/skills + GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/skills/:id. Admin auth + CSRF + 64KiB body cap + zod. PluginError to HTTP-status mapping per plan. Wire registerAdminSkillsRoutes into plugin init.

content.subject: Task 1.3.2: channel-web typed wire client (lib/skills.ts)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/skills.ts mirroring lib/credentials.ts. Exports listSkills/getSkill/upsertSkill/updateSkill/deleteSkill. Add @ax/skills workspace dep to channel-web package.json.

content.subject: Task 1.4.1: agents skill_attachments column + Agent type
content.description: Add ALTER TABLE agents_v1_agents ADD COLUMN skill_attachments JSONB. Extend Agent type with skillAttachments: SkillAttachment[]. Update store read/write. Audit AgentRecord literal usages.

content.subject: Task 1.4.2: agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook
content.description: Register agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook in @ax/agents plugin. JSONB containment query against skill_attachments column.

content.subject: Task 1.4.3: PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments
content.description: Add PATCH route in @ax/agents admin-routes with attach-time slot-collision detection. Errors: skill-not-found, binding-missing, binding-orphan, slot-collision. Factor validateNewAttachments() as pure helper.

content.subject: Task 1.5.1: Extend orchestrator AgentRecord + OpenSessionInput
content.description: Add skillAttachments to chat-orchestrator AgentRecord (structural duplicate per I2). Add installedSkills?: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] to OpenSessionInput. Fix test fixtures.

content.subject: Task 1.5.2: Orchestrator union step + 3 new outcomes
content.description: In chat-orchestrator before proxy:open-session: resolve attached skills, union allowedHosts, merge credentialBindings. Surface skill-binding-missing/skill-slot-collision/skill-resolve-failed outcomes. Thread installedSkills into sandbox:open-session.

content.subject: Task 1.5.3: sandbox-subprocess materialize installed skills
content.description: Add installedSkills field to OpenSessionInput. Write each skill to $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md (mode 0444). chmod 0555 parent dir AFTER all writes. Validate skill id regex.

content.subject: Task 1.5.4: sandbox-k8s + runner AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON
content.description: Add installedSkills to k8s pod-spec as AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON env var (256KiB cap). Add to runner's ENV_ALLOWLIST (regression test). Runner main.ts materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv() called before SDK spawn. chmod 0555 parent after writes.

content.subject: Task 1.6.1: Skills entry in channel-web admin nav
content.description: Add 'skills' to AdminView union in lib/admin.ts and nav entry in AdminSidebar.tsx.

content.subject: Task 1.6.2: SkillsTab (list + create + delete)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx using shadcn Card/Table/Dialog/Alert. List, +New skill, Edit, Delete with confirm. Surface 409 attached errors.

content.subject: Task 1.6.3: SkillEditor (textarea + live preview)
content.description: Two-pane SkillEditor.tsx — Textarea + live-parsed preview using parseSkillManifest. Save → upsertSkill/updateSkill with CSRF.

content.subject: Task 1.6.4: SkillAttachmentsSection inside AgentForm
content.description: Create SkillAttachmentsSection.tsx — list attachments, +Attach skill picker, slot-binding sub-form with credential dropdown filtered by kind. Embed in AgentForm. Make allowed-hosts a read-only computed view.

content.subject: Task 1.7.1: Preset wiring (CLI + k8s)
content.description: Load @ax/skills in packages/cli/src/main.ts BEFORE @ax/agents. Same in presets/k8s/src/index.ts. Update preset.test.ts load-list (NOT PLUGINS_TO_DROP). Update package.json deps.

content.subject: Task 1.7.2: Canary acceptance + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
content.description: Add install→attach→chat canary scenario. Test: happy path, SDK Skill tool fires, skills:delete blocked, skill-binding-missing termination outcome. Extend MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md with one new bullet.

content.subject: Task 1.7.3: Open PR with invariant audit
content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Open PR with title 'feat(skills): Phase 1 — installed-skills workflow' and body containing I-P1-1..8 audit, half-wired window CLOSED, boundary review, test plan, follow-ups.

content.text:
Now wire it in BEFORE `createAgentsPlugin` so `agents:any-attached-to-skill` is loaded when skills:delete checks fire. Actually it's the other way — skills:delete checks agents:any-attached-to-skill, which is registered by @ax/agents. So skills can load AFTER agents — they're soft-coupled via bus.hasService.

But the plan says push BEFORE createAgentsPlugin. Let me read the plan again... actually the plan says "push BEFORE `createAgentsPlugin` so `agents:any-attached-to-skill` is registered when delete checks fire". That's backwards — if skills:delete runs at RUNTIME, by then both are init'd. The kernel sorts via manifest.calls topology. Loading order doesn't matter for runtime.

I'll load skills right after agents for clarity (skills consumes agents:any-attached-to-skill conceptually).

content.subject: Task 1.1.1: Scaffold @ax/skills package
content.description: Create package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, src/types.ts for @ax/skills. Add to workspace and root tsconfig refs. Verify build is clean. Commit.

content.subject: Task 1.1.2: Manifest parser + capability schema
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/manifest.ts with parseSkillManifest(). Schema: name kebab-case, description ≤240 chars, allowedHosts (no scheme/path/wildcard/IP), credentials with SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE slots, kind enum, inline-secret rejection at any depth, mcpServers reserved.

content.subject: Task 1.1.3: Strip capabilities from workspace SKILL.md (validator-skill)
content.description: Add stripCapabilitiesFromFrontmatter helper to packages/validator-skill/src/frontmatter.ts. Wire into workspace:pre-apply. Determine rewrite vs veto by reading core. Either strip-and-warn or veto.

content.subject: Task 1.2.1: skills_v1_skills migration
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/migrations.ts with runSkillsMigration() that creates skills_v1_skills table with columns: skill_id PRIMARY KEY, description, manifest_yaml, body_md, version, created_at, updated_at.

content.subject: Task 1.2.2: SkillsStore CRUD
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/store.ts with createSkillsStore(db) exposing list/get/upsert/delete/resolve. Capabilities re-parsed from manifest_yaml on read. resolve preserves input order, drops unknown ids.

content.subject: Task 1.2.3: @ax/skills plugin (5 service hooks)
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/plugin.ts with createSkillsPlugin() registering skills:list/get/upsert/delete/resolve service hooks. skills:delete checks agents:any-attached-to-skill structurally.

content.subject: Task 1.3.1: Admin HTTP routes for /admin/skills
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts. GET/POST /admin/skills + GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/skills/:id. Admin auth + CSRF + 64KiB body cap + zod. PluginError to HTTP-status mapping per plan. Wire registerAdminSkillsRoutes into plugin init.

content.subject: Task 1.3.2: channel-web typed wire client (lib/skills.ts)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/skills.ts mirroring lib/credentials.ts. Exports listSkills/getSkill/upsertSkill/updateSkill/deleteSkill. Add @ax/skills workspace dep to channel-web package.json.

content.subject: Task 1.4.1: agents skill_attachments column + Agent type
content.description: Add ALTER TABLE agents_v1_agents ADD COLUMN skill_attachments JSONB. Extend Agent type with skillAttachments: SkillAttachment[]. Update store read/write. Audit AgentRecord literal usages.

content.subject: Task 1.4.2: agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook
content.description: Register agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook in @ax/agents plugin. JSONB containment query against skill_attachments column.

content.subject: Task 1.4.3: PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments
content.description: Add PATCH route in @ax/agents admin-routes with attach-time slot-collision detection. Errors: skill-not-found, binding-missing, binding-orphan, slot-collision. Factor validateNewAttachments() as pure helper.

content.subject: Task 1.5.1: Extend orchestrator AgentRecord + OpenSessionInput
content.description: Add skillAttachments to chat-orchestrator AgentRecord (structural duplicate per I2). Add installedSkills?: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] to OpenSessionInput. Fix test fixtures.

content.subject: Task 1.5.2: Orchestrator union step + 3 new outcomes
content.description: In chat-orchestrator before proxy:open-session: resolve attached skills, union allowedHosts, merge credentialBindings. Surface skill-binding-missing/skill-slot-collision/skill-resolve-failed outcomes. Thread installedSkills into sandbox:open-session.

content.subject: Task 1.5.3: sandbox-subprocess materialize installed skills
content.description: Add installedSkills field to OpenSessionInput. Write each skill to $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md (mode 0444). chmod 0555 parent dir AFTER all writes. Validate skill id regex.

content.subject: Task 1.5.4: sandbox-k8s + runner AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON
content.description: Add installedSkills to k8s pod-spec as AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON env var (256KiB cap). Add to runner's ENV_ALLOWLIST (regression test). Runner main.ts materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv() called before SDK spawn. chmod 0555 parent after writes.

content.subject: Task 1.6.1: Skills entry in channel-web admin nav
content.description: Add 'skills' to AdminView union in lib/admin.ts and nav entry in AdminSidebar.tsx.

content.subject: Task 1.6.2: SkillsTab (list + create + delete)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx using shadcn Card/Table/Dialog/Alert. List, +New skill, Edit, Delete with confirm. Surface 409 attached errors.

content.subject: Task 1.6.3: SkillEditor (textarea + live preview)
content.description: Two-pane SkillEditor.tsx — Textarea + live-parsed preview using parseSkillManifest. Save → upsertSkill/updateSkill with CSRF.

content.subject: Task 1.6.4: SkillAttachmentsSection inside AgentForm
content.description: Create SkillAttachmentsSection.tsx — list attachments, +Attach skill picker, slot-binding sub-form with credential dropdown filtered by kind. Embed in AgentForm. Make allowed-hosts a read-only computed view.

content.subject: Task 1.7.1: Preset wiring (CLI + k8s)
content.description: Load @ax/skills in packages/cli/src/main.ts BEFORE @ax/agents. Same in presets/k8s/src/index.ts. Update preset.test.ts load-list (NOT PLUGINS_TO_DROP). Update package.json deps.

content.subject: Task 1.7.2: Canary acceptance + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
content.description: Add install→attach→chat canary scenario. Test: happy path, SDK Skill tool fires, skills:delete blocked, skill-binding-missing termination outcome. Extend MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md with one new bullet.

content.subject: Task 1.7.3: Open PR with invariant audit
content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Open PR with title 'feat(skills): Phase 1 — installed-skills workflow' and body containing I-P1-1..8 audit, half-wired window CLOSED, boundary review, test plan, follow-ups.

content.subject: Task 1.4.2: agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook
content.description: Register agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook in @ax/agents plugin. JSONB containment query against skill_attachments column.

content.subject: Task 1.4.3: PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments
content.description: Add PATCH route in @ax/agents admin-routes with attach-time slot-collision detection. Errors: skill-not-found, binding-missing, binding-orphan, slot-collision. Factor validateNewAttachments() as pure helper.

content.subject: Task 1.5.1: Extend orchestrator AgentRecord + OpenSessionInput
content.description: Add skillAttachments to chat-orchestrator AgentRecord (structural duplicate per I2). Add installedSkills?: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] to OpenSessionInput. Fix test fixtures.

content.subject: Task 1.5.2: Orchestrator union step + 3 new outcomes
content.description: In chat-orchestrator before proxy:open-session: resolve attached skills, union allowedHosts, merge credentialBindings. Surface skill-binding-missing/skill-slot-collision/skill-resolve-failed outcomes. Thread installedSkills into sandbox:open-session.

content.subject: Task 1.5.3: sandbox-subprocess materialize installed skills
content.description: Add installedSkills field to OpenSessionInput. Write each skill to $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md (mode 0444). chmod 0555 parent dir AFTER all writes. Validate skill id regex.

content.subject: Task 1.5.4: sandbox-k8s + runner AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON
content.description: Add installedSkills to k8s pod-spec as AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON env var (256KiB cap). Add to runner's ENV_ALLOWLIST (regression test). Runner main.ts materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv() called before SDK spawn. chmod 0555 parent after writes.

content.subject: Task 1.6.1: Skills entry in channel-web admin nav
content.description: Add 'skills' to AdminView union in lib/admin.ts and nav entry in AdminSidebar.tsx.

content.subject: Task 1.6.2: SkillsTab (list + create + delete)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx using shadcn Card/Table/Dialog/Alert. List, +New skill, Edit, Delete with confirm. Surface 409 attached errors.

content.subject: Task 1.6.3: SkillEditor (textarea + live preview)
content.description: Two-pane SkillEditor.tsx — Textarea + live-parsed preview using parseSkillManifest. Save → upsertSkill/updateSkill with CSRF.

content.subject: Task 1.6.4: SkillAttachmentsSection inside AgentForm
content.description: Create SkillAttachmentsSection.tsx — list attachments, +Attach skill picker, slot-binding sub-form with credential dropdown filtered by kind. Embed in AgentForm. Make allowed-hosts a read-only computed view.

content.subject: Task 1.7.1: Preset wiring (CLI + k8s)
content.description: Load @ax/skills in packages/cli/src/main.ts BEFORE @ax/agents. Same in presets/k8s/src/index.ts. Update preset.test.ts load-list (NOT PLUGINS_TO_DROP). Update package.json deps.

content.subject: Task 1.7.2: Canary acceptance + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
content.description: Add install→attach→chat canary scenario. Test: happy path, SDK Skill tool fires, skills:delete blocked, skill-binding-missing termination outcome. Extend MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md with one new bullet.

input.new_string:
## Scenario: Install a skill, attach to agent, chat (Phase 1 skill-install)

### What this proves

Admin installs a skill via /admin/skills, attaches it to an agent (binding
its credential slots to existing credentials), opens a chat, and the runner
sees the SKILL.md materialized + the proxy substitutes the bound credential
when the model calls the skill's allowedHost. Validates I-P1-1..8 in a
real cluster.

### Walk

1. Sign in as admin. Navigate to Admin → Credentials. Add a credential
   `kind=api-key`, `scope=global`, `ref=gh-pat`, with any string for value
   (the bytes don't have to be a real PAT for this walk — the proxy will
   substitute and the upstream will 401, which is fine for the gate check).
2. Navigate to Admin → Skills (new tab in the sidebar). Click `+ New skill`.
   Paste:
   ```yaml
   ---
   name: github
   description: Access the GitHub REST API with a personal access token.
   capabilities:
     allowedHosts:
       - api.github.com
     credentials:
       - slot: GITHUB_TOKEN
         kind: api-key
         description: GitHub PAT.
   ---
   Use this skill to call the GitHub REST API.
   ```
   The live preview pane should show `github`, the allowedHost chip
   `api.github.com`, and the slot `GITHUB_TOKEN (api-key)`. Click Install.
3. Confirm `github` appears in the Skills table with the host and slot
   chips populated.
4. Navigate to Admin → Agents, pick any test agent, scroll to the Skills
   section. Click `+ Attach skill`, pick `github`. A slot-binding row
   appears: `GITHUB_TOKEN ← [Select credential ▾]`. Pick `gh-pat`. Click
   `Save attachments`.
5. Open a chat with that agent and ask something benign: "Try to fetch
   https://api.github.com/users/torvalds and tell me the response status."
6. Verify in the network panel that the agent's tool call to api.github.com
   was permitted by the proxy (the proxy substitutes the placeholder with
   the credential value). The upstream may return 401 if the PAT is fake —
   that's fine; we're checking that the proxy DIDN'T return 403 (allowedHost
   gate) and that the substitution happened (no placeholder leaked to GitHub).
7. Navigate back to Admin → Skills. Click Delete on the `github` row.
   Verify the API returns 409 with "skill is attached" — the delete is
   blocked because the agent still has it attached.
8. Detach the skill from the agent (Skills section → trash icon → Save).
   Retry delete in the Skills tab. Verify it succeeds (204).
9. (Optional) `kubectl exec` into the runner pod for the session you opened
   in step 5 and confirm `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/github/SKILL.md` exists
   with mode 0444, and the parent `skills/` dir is mode 0555.

### Acceptance criteria

- Skill installs via UI; appears in the table with host + slot chips.
- Attach + save succeeds; the agent record gains a
  `skill_attachments[].skillId='github'` row.
- Chat session opens without termination outcomes (skill-resolve-failed,
  skill-binding-missing, skill-slot-collision are all clear).
- Proxy permits the api.github.com tool call (no 403 from the proxy).
- skills:delete is blocked when the skill is attached (409); succeeds
  after detach (204).
- (Optional cluster-side) SKILL.md exists at the expected path inside
  the runner pod with the expected modes.

### Cleanup

```bash
# Delete the test agent + the github skill + the gh-pat credential
# via the admin UI. All three are scoped to this walk and cheap to recreate.
```

## When this passes, do
1. Update the PR description's acceptance section with the date + cluster
   used + a copy of the `psql` count outputs.
2. Mark Task 21 complete.

```bash

aiTitle: Implement skill install phase 1 with subagent

content.text:
Now the SkillEditor cross-plugin import issue. The eslint rule allows `import type`, but SkillEditor uses `parseSkillManifest` as a value. The solution: move `parseSkillManifest` into channel-web (duplicate it) OR... actually, the cleaner approach is to add `@ax/skills/manifest` to the allowlist since the manifest module is pure-function + the agreed wire shape that the UI needs to preview.

Let me check the eslint rule:

content.content:
31://   @ax/validator-routine — pure-function routine frontmatter parser
111:          patterns: [
121:                '!@ax/validator-routine',
125:                'Cross-plugin runtime imports are forbidden. Plugins communicate through the hook bus only. See CLAUDE.md invariant 2. Type-only imports (`import type {...}` / `export type {...}`) are allowed — boundary types are how plugins agree on a shared contract without runtime coupling. The only @ax/* runtime imports allowed in plugin code are @ax/core, @ax/test-harness, @ax/ipc-protocol + @ax/workspace-protocol (wire schemas), @ax/ipc-core (transport-agnostic IPC library), @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host (pure-function jsonl→Turn[] parser), and @ax/validator-routine (pure-function routine frontmatter parser shared between the validator and the routines plugin).',

content.subject: Task 1.1.1: Scaffold @ax/skills package
content.description: Create package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, src/types.ts for @ax/skills. Add to workspace and root tsconfig refs. Verify build is clean. Commit.

content.subject: Task 1.1.2: Manifest parser + capability schema
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/manifest.ts with parseSkillManifest(). Schema: name kebab-case, description ≤240 chars, allowedHosts (no scheme/path/wildcard/IP), credentials with SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE slots, kind enum, inline-secret rejection at any depth, mcpServers reserved.

content.subject: Task 1.1.3: Strip capabilities from workspace SKILL.md (validator-skill)
content.description: Add stripCapabilitiesFromFrontmatter helper to packages/validator-skill/src/frontmatter.ts. Wire into workspace:pre-apply. Determine rewrite vs veto by reading core. Either strip-and-warn or veto.

content.subject: Task 1.2.1: skills_v1_skills migration
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/migrations.ts with runSkillsMigration() that creates skills_v1_skills table with columns: skill_id PRIMARY KEY, description, manifest_yaml, body_md, version, created_at, updated_at.

content.subject: Task 1.2.2: SkillsStore CRUD
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/store.ts with createSkillsStore(db) exposing list/get/upsert/delete/resolve. Capabilities re-parsed from manifest_yaml on read. resolve preserves input order, drops unknown ids.

content.subject: Task 1.2.3: @ax/skills plugin (5 service hooks)
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/plugin.ts with createSkillsPlugin() registering skills:list/get/upsert/delete/resolve service hooks. skills:delete checks agents:any-attached-to-skill structurally.

content.subject: Task 1.3.1: Admin HTTP routes for /admin/skills
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts. GET/POST /admin/skills + GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/skills/:id. Admin auth + CSRF + 64KiB body cap + zod. PluginError to HTTP-status mapping per plan. Wire registerAdminSkillsRoutes into plugin init.

content.subject: Task 1.3.2: channel-web typed wire client (lib/skills.ts)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/skills.ts mirroring lib/credentials.ts. Exports listSkills/getSkill/upsertSkill/updateSkill/deleteSkill. Add @ax/skills workspace dep to channel-web package.json.

content.subject: Task 1.4.1: agents skill_attachments column + Agent type
content.description: Add ALTER TABLE agents_v1_agents ADD COLUMN skill_attachments JSONB. Extend Agent type with skillAttachments: SkillAttachment[]. Update store read/write. Audit AgentRecord literal usages.

content.subject: Task 1.4.2: agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook
content.description: Register agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook in @ax/agents plugin. JSONB containment query against skill_attachments column.

content.subject: Task 1.4.3: PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments
content.description: Add PATCH route in @ax/agents admin-routes with attach-time slot-collision detection. Errors: skill-not-found, binding-missing, binding-orphan, slot-collision. Factor validateNewAttachments() as pure helper.

content.subject: Task 1.5.1: Extend orchestrator AgentRecord + OpenSessionInput
content.description: Add skillAttachments to chat-orchestrator AgentRecord (structural duplicate per I2). Add installedSkills?: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] to OpenSessionInput. Fix test fixtures.

content.subject: Task 1.5.2: Orchestrator union step + 3 new outcomes
content.description: In chat-orchestrator before proxy:open-session: resolve attached skills, union allowedHosts, merge credentialBindings. Surface skill-binding-missing/skill-slot-collision/skill-resolve-failed outcomes. Thread installedSkills into sandbox:open-session.

content.subject: Task 1.5.3: sandbox-subprocess materialize installed skills
content.description: Add installedSkills field to OpenSessionInput. Write each skill to $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md (mode 0444). chmod 0555 parent dir AFTER all writes. Validate skill id regex.

content.subject: Task 1.5.4: sandbox-k8s + runner AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON
content.description: Add installedSkills to k8s pod-spec as AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON env var (256KiB cap). Add to runner's ENV_ALLOWLIST (regression test). Runner main.ts materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv() called before SDK spawn. chmod 0555 parent after writes.

content.subject: Task 1.6.1: Skills entry in channel-web admin nav
content.description: Add 'skills' to AdminView union in lib/admin.ts and nav entry in AdminSidebar.tsx.

content.subject: Task 1.6.2: SkillsTab (list + create + delete)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx using shadcn Card/Table/Dialog/Alert. List, +New skill, Edit, Delete with confirm. Surface 409 attached errors.

content.subject: Task 1.6.3: SkillEditor (textarea + live preview)
content.description: Two-pane SkillEditor.tsx — Textarea + live-parsed preview using parseSkillManifest. Save → upsertSkill/updateSkill with CSRF.

content.subject: Task 1.6.4: SkillAttachmentsSection inside AgentForm
content.description: Create SkillAttachmentsSection.tsx — list attachments, +Attach skill picker, slot-binding sub-form with credential dropdown filtered by kind. Embed in AgentForm. Make allowed-hosts a read-only computed view.

content.subject: Task 1.7.1: Preset wiring (CLI + k8s)
content.description: Load @ax/skills in packages/cli/src/main.ts BEFORE @ax/agents. Same in presets/k8s/src/index.ts. Update preset.test.ts load-list (NOT PLUGINS_TO_DROP). Update package.json deps.

content.subject: Task 1.7.2: Canary acceptance + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
content.description: Add install→attach→chat canary scenario. Test: happy path, SDK Skill tool fires, skills:delete blocked, skill-binding-missing termination outcome. Extend MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md with one new bullet.

content.subject: Task 1.7.3: Open PR with invariant audit
content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Open PR with title 'feat(skills): Phase 1 — installed-skills workflow' and body containing I-P1-1..8 audit, half-wired window CLOSED, boundary review, test plan, follow-ups.

content.subject: Task 1.1.1: Scaffold @ax/skills package
content.description: Create package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, src/types.ts for @ax/skills. Add to workspace and root tsconfig refs. Verify build is clean. Commit.

content.subject: Task 1.1.2: Manifest parser + capability schema
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/manifest.ts with parseSkillManifest(). Schema: name kebab-case, description ≤240 chars, allowedHosts (no scheme/path/wildcard/IP), credentials with SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE slots, kind enum, inline-secret rejection at any depth, mcpServers reserved.

content.subject: Task 1.1.3: Strip capabilities from workspace SKILL.md (validator-skill)
content.description: Add stripCapabilitiesFromFrontmatter helper to packages/validator-skill/src/frontmatter.ts. Wire into workspace:pre-apply. Determine rewrite vs veto by reading core. Either strip-and-warn or veto.

content.subject: Task 1.2.1: skills_v1_skills migration
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/migrations.ts with runSkillsMigration() that creates skills_v1_skills table with columns: skill_id PRIMARY KEY, description, manifest_yaml, body_md, version, created_at, updated_at.

content.subject: Task 1.2.2: SkillsStore CRUD
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/store.ts with createSkillsStore(db) exposing list/get/upsert/delete/resolve. Capabilities re-parsed from manifest_yaml on read. resolve preserves input order, drops unknown ids.

content.subject: Task 1.2.3: @ax/skills plugin (5 service hooks)
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/plugin.ts with createSkillsPlugin() registering skills:list/get/upsert/delete/resolve service hooks. skills:delete checks agents:any-attached-to-skill structurally.

content.subject: Task 1.3.1: Admin HTTP routes for /admin/skills
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts. GET/POST /admin/skills + GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/skills/:id. Admin auth + CSRF + 64KiB body cap + zod. PluginError to HTTP-status mapping per plan. Wire registerAdminSkillsRoutes into plugin init.

content.subject: Task 1.3.2: channel-web typed wire client (lib/skills.ts)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/skills.ts mirroring lib/credentials.ts. Exports listSkills/getSkill/upsertSkill/updateSkill/deleteSkill. Add @ax/skills workspace dep to channel-web package.json.

content.subject: Task 1.4.1: agents skill_attachments column + Agent type
content.description: Add ALTER TABLE agents_v1_agents ADD COLUMN skill_attachments JSONB. Extend Agent type with skillAttachments: SkillAttachment[]. Update store read/write. Audit AgentRecord literal usages.

content.subject: Task 1.4.2: agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook
content.description: Register agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook in @ax/agents plugin. JSONB containment query against skill_attachments column.

content.subject: Task 1.4.3: PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments
content.description: Add PATCH route in @ax/agents admin-routes with attach-time slot-collision detection. Errors: skill-not-found, binding-missing, binding-orphan, slot-collision. Factor validateNewAttachments() as pure helper.

content.subject: Task 1.5.1: Extend orchestrator AgentRecord + OpenSessionInput
content.description: Add skillAttachments to chat-orchestrator AgentRecord (structural duplicate per I2). Add installedSkills?: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] to OpenSessionInput. Fix test fixtures.

content.subject: Task 1.5.2: Orchestrator union step + 3 new outcomes
content.description: In chat-orchestrator before proxy:open-session: resolve attached skills, union allowedHosts, merge credentialBindings. Surface skill-binding-missing/skill-slot-collision/skill-resolve-failed outcomes. Thread installedSkills into sandbox:open-session.

content.subject: Task 1.5.3: sandbox-subprocess materialize installed skills
content.description: Add installedSkills field to OpenSessionInput. Write each skill to $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md (mode 0444). chmod 0555 parent dir AFTER all writes. Validate skill id regex.

content.subject: Task 1.5.4: sandbox-k8s + runner AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON
content.description: Add installedSkills to k8s pod-spec as AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON env var (256KiB cap). Add to runner's ENV_ALLOWLIST (regression test). Runner main.ts materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv() called before SDK spawn. chmod 0555 parent after writes.

content.subject: Task 1.6.1: Skills entry in channel-web admin nav
content.description: Add 'skills' to AdminView union in lib/admin.ts and nav entry in AdminSidebar.tsx.

content.subject: Task 1.6.2: SkillsTab (list + create + delete)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx using shadcn Card/Table/Dialog/Alert. List, +New skill, Edit, Delete with confirm. Surface 409 attached errors.

content.subject: Task 1.6.3: SkillEditor (textarea + live preview)
content.description: Two-pane SkillEditor.tsx — Textarea + live-parsed preview using parseSkillManifest. Save → upsertSkill/updateSkill with CSRF.

content.subject: Task 1.6.4: SkillAttachmentsSection inside AgentForm
content.description: Create SkillAttachmentsSection.tsx — list attachments, +Attach skill picker, slot-binding sub-form with credential dropdown filtered by kind. Embed in AgentForm. Make allowed-hosts a read-only computed view.

content.subject: Task 1.7.1: Preset wiring (CLI + k8s)
content.description: Load @ax/skills in packages/cli/src/main.ts BEFORE @ax/agents. Same in presets/k8s/src/index.ts. Update preset.test.ts load-list (NOT PLUGINS_TO_DROP). Update package.json deps.

content.subject: Task 1.7.2: Canary acceptance + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
content.description: Add install→attach→chat canary scenario. Test: happy path, SDK Skill tool fires, skills:delete blocked, skill-binding-missing termination outcome. Extend MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md with one new bullet.

content.subject: Task 1.7.3: Open PR with invariant audit
content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Open PR with title 'feat(skills): Phase 1 — installed-skills workflow' and body containing I-P1-1..8 audit, half-wired window CLOSED, boundary review, test plan, follow-ups.

content.text: Picomatch in the no-restricted-imports rule matches subpaths against the broader `@ax/*` pattern. The cleanest fix: inline eslint-disable with the same justification we added to the allowlist comment.

content.subject: Task 1.1.1: Scaffold @ax/skills package
content.description: Create package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, src/types.ts for @ax/skills. Add to workspace and root tsconfig refs. Verify build is clean. Commit.

content.subject: Task 1.1.2: Manifest parser + capability schema
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/manifest.ts with parseSkillManifest(). Schema: name kebab-case, description ≤240 chars, allowedHosts (no scheme/path/wildcard/IP), credentials with SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE slots, kind enum, inline-secret rejection at any depth, mcpServers reserved.

content.subject: Task 1.1.3: Strip capabilities from workspace SKILL.md (validator-skill)
content.description: Add stripCapabilitiesFromFrontmatter helper to packages/validator-skill/src/frontmatter.ts. Wire into workspace:pre-apply. Determine rewrite vs veto by reading core. Either strip-and-warn or veto.

content.subject: Task 1.2.1: skills_v1_skills migration
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/migrations.ts with runSkillsMigration() that creates skills_v1_skills table with columns: skill_id PRIMARY KEY, description, manifest_yaml, body_md, version, created_at, updated_at.

content.subject: Task 1.2.2: SkillsStore CRUD
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/store.ts with createSkillsStore(db) exposing list/get/upsert/delete/resolve. Capabilities re-parsed from manifest_yaml on read. resolve preserves input order, drops unknown ids.

content.subject: Task 1.2.3: @ax/skills plugin (5 service hooks)
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/plugin.ts with createSkillsPlugin() registering skills:list/get/upsert/delete/resolve service hooks. skills:delete checks agents:any-attached-to-skill structurally.

content.subject: Task 1.3.1: Admin HTTP routes for /admin/skills
content.description: Create packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts. GET/POST /admin/skills + GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/skills/:id. Admin auth + CSRF + 64KiB body cap + zod. PluginError to HTTP-status mapping per plan. Wire registerAdminSkillsRoutes into plugin init.

content.subject: Task 1.3.2: channel-web typed wire client (lib/skills.ts)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/skills.ts mirroring lib/credentials.ts. Exports listSkills/getSkill/upsertSkill/updateSkill/deleteSkill. Add @ax/skills workspace dep to channel-web package.json.

content.subject: Task 1.4.1: agents skill_attachments column + Agent type
content.description: Add ALTER TABLE agents_v1_agents ADD COLUMN skill_attachments JSONB. Extend Agent type with skillAttachments: SkillAttachment[]. Update store read/write. Audit AgentRecord literal usages.

content.subject: Task 1.4.2: agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook
content.description: Register agents:any-attached-to-skill service hook in @ax/agents plugin. JSONB containment query against skill_attachments column.

content.subject: Task 1.4.3: PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments
content.description: Add PATCH route in @ax/agents admin-routes with attach-time slot-collision detection. Errors: skill-not-found, binding-missing, binding-orphan, slot-collision. Factor validateNewAttachments() as pure helper.

content.subject: Task 1.5.1: Extend orchestrator AgentRecord + OpenSessionInput
content.description: Add skillAttachments to chat-orchestrator AgentRecord (structural duplicate per I2). Add installedSkills?: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] to OpenSessionInput. Fix test fixtures.

content.subject: Task 1.5.2: Orchestrator union step + 3 new outcomes
content.description: In chat-orchestrator before proxy:open-session: resolve attached skills, union allowedHosts, merge credentialBindings. Surface skill-binding-missing/skill-slot-collision/skill-resolve-failed outcomes. Thread installedSkills into sandbox:open-session.

content.subject: Task 1.5.3: sandbox-subprocess materialize installed skills
content.description: Add installedSkills field to OpenSessionInput. Write each skill to $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md (mode 0444). chmod 0555 parent dir AFTER all writes. Validate skill id regex.

content.subject: Task 1.5.4: sandbox-k8s + runner AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON
content.description: Add installedSkills to k8s pod-spec as AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON env var (256KiB cap). Add to runner's ENV_ALLOWLIST (regression test). Runner main.ts materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv() called before SDK spawn. chmod 0555 parent after writes.

content.subject: Task 1.6.1: Skills entry in channel-web admin nav
content.description: Add 'skills' to AdminView union in lib/admin.ts and nav entry in AdminSidebar.tsx.

content.subject: Task 1.6.2: SkillsTab (list + create + delete)
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx using shadcn Card/Table/Dialog/Alert. List, +New skill, Edit, Delete with confirm. Surface 409 attached errors.

content.subject: Task 1.6.3: SkillEditor (textarea + live preview)
content.description: Two-pane SkillEditor.tsx — Textarea + live-parsed preview using parseSkillManifest. Save → upsertSkill/updateSkill with CSRF.

content.subject: Task 1.6.4: SkillAttachmentsSection inside AgentForm
content.description: Create SkillAttachmentsSection.tsx — list attachments, +Attach skill picker, slot-binding sub-form with credential dropdown filtered by kind. Embed in AgentForm. Make allowed-hosts a read-only computed view.

content.subject: Task 1.7.1: Preset wiring (CLI + k8s)
content.description: Load @ax/skills in packages/cli/src/main.ts BEFORE @ax/agents. Same in presets/k8s/src/index.ts. Update preset.test.ts load-list (NOT PLUGINS_TO_DROP). Update package.json deps.

content.subject: Task 1.7.2: Canary acceptance + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
content.description: Add install→attach→chat canary scenario. Test: happy path, SDK Skill tool fires, skills:delete blocked, skill-binding-missing termination outcome. Extend MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md with one new bullet.

content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Open PR with title 'feat(skills): Phase 1 — installed-skills workflow' and body containing I-P1-1..8 audit, half-wired window CLOSED, boundary review, test plan, follow-ups.

aiTitle: Implement skill install phase 1 with subagent

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(skills): Phase 1 — installed-skills workflow (manifest + DB + admin UI + orchestrator union)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Phase 1 of the skill-install workflow ships the host-managed installed-skills end-to-end on top of Phase 0's SDK setting-source wiring (PR #95): a new `@ax/skills` plugin (manifest schema with `capabilities` block + DB-backed storage + admin HTTP CRUD), a `skillAttachments` JSONB column on agents, orchestrator integration that unions skill-declared `allowedHosts` and merges credential bindings into `proxy:open-session`, sandbox materialization of installed-skill `SKILL.md` files into `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/` (chmod 0555 after write), and a channel-web admin UI tab + per-agent Skills section.

End-to-end manual canary in `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`: admin installs a `github` skill → admin attaches to an agent + binds `GITHUB_TOKEN` slot → user chats with the agent → a request to `api.github.com` lands with the MITM-substituted token.

## Phase 1 invariants

- **I-P1-1** — Manifest validator hardened. Schema validation runs on every `skills:upsert`. Inline-secret rejection at any depth (`apiKey`/`token`/`password`/`secret`) → `inline-secret-forbidden`. Reserved `capabilities.mcpServers` → `capability-deferred`. `allowedHosts` rejects schemes/paths/wildcards/IP literals. Slot names enforce `/^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]{0,63}$/`, unique within manifest. `kind` restricted to `{'api-key'}`. Caught at admin route (400) BEFORE any DB write. **Test:** `packages/skills/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts` (16 tests) + `admin-routes.test.ts` (19 tests).
- **I-P1-2** — Workspace SKILL.md `capabilities` block is strip-and-warn, never honor. Agent-authored writes through `workspace:pre-apply` cannot grant new hosts or credentials. The SDK-config-paths veto from Phase 0 continues unchanged. **Test:** `packages/validator-skill/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` + `frontmatter.test.ts`.
- **I-P1-3** — Installed-skill SKILL.md bodies materialize under `$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills/<id>/SKILL.md` (mode 0444) with parent skills-dir chmod 0555 AFTER all writes. Subprocess sandbox writes in-process; k8s passes `AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON` env to the runner which materializes server-side before SDK spawn. **Test:** `packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/__tests__/open-session.test.ts` + `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/installed-skills.test.ts` (11 tests).
- **I-P1-4** — Slot-name collision detected at **attach time** (admin route 400 with `slot-collision`) AND at **session-open** (orchestrator emits `kind: 'terminated', reason: 'skill-slot-collision'`) as data-drift safety net. **Test:** `packages/agents/src/__tests__/skill-attachments-validation.test.ts` (8 pure-fn tests) + `packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts` (9 new tests).
- **I-P1-5** — Orchestrator unions `allowedHosts` (set-dedup) and merges `credentialBindings` (per-slot) into `proxy:open-session`. Three new termination outcomes ride alongside `proxy-not-loaded`/`agent-proxy-config-incomplete`: `skill-binding-missing`, `skill-slot-collision`, `skill-resolve-failed`. **Test:** orchestrator.test.ts new describe block.
- **I-P1-6** — `skills:delete` blocked (HTTP 409, code `skill-in-use`) when any agent has the skill attached. `agents:any-attached-to-skill` is the structural check; soft-coupled via `bus.hasService` so stripped presets without `@ax/agents` still load `@ax/skills`. **Test:** plugin.test.ts + admin-routes.test.ts (delete-blocked case with stub).
- **I-P1-7** — Half-wired window CLOSED in this PR. K8s preset loads `@ax/skills` between `@ax/agents` and `@ax/credentials-admin-routes`. CLI preset intentionally does NOT load it (sqlite vs postgres-DDL gap, matching the existing `@ax/routines`/`@ax/conversations`/`@ax/attachments` pattern). MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md has a new scenario covering the install→attach→chat workflow on a real cluster.
- **I-P1-8** — One UI design language. Admin UI lives in `packages/channel-web`, composes existing shadcn primitives + semantic tokens (CLAUDE.md invariant #6). New shadcn primitive installed: `Table`. No new Vite SPA. No hand-rolled forms. No raw colors.

## Half-wired window — CLOSED

- **New plugin:** `@ax/skills` (manifest parser + storage + 5 service hooks + 5 admin routes).
- **Preset wiring:** loaded in `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` between `@ax/agents` and `@ax/credentials-admin-routes`; the production deploy assembly references it. NOT loaded in `packages/cli/src/main.ts` (sqlite preset; same exclusion pattern as `@ax/routines` / `@ax/conversations` / `@ax/attachments`).
- **Reach-test:** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/preset.test.ts` "expected production plugin set" assertion lists `@ax/skills`; the two `acceptance.test.ts` files (chat-path + multi-tenant) add it to `PLUGINS_TO_DROP` for the sqlite-stub canaries — same handling as `@ax/agents`.
- **End-to-end manual canary:** `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Install a skill, attach to agent, chat" section.

## Boundary review

- **Alternate impl for `skills:*` hooks:** `@ax/skills-fs` (filesystem-backed sibling reading SKILL.md from a host-controlled directory). The payload shapes (`SkillSummary` / `SkillDetail` / `ResolvedSkill` / the 5 input/output types) name no storage detail. `SkillsRow` lives only inside `@ax/skills/src/migrations.ts`.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `manifestYaml` + `bodyMd` are content, not row internals. `version` is a manifest-declared integer (model versioning), not a row schema version.
- **Subscriber risk:** none added in this PR. Service-hook-only.
- **Wire surface (IPC actions):** `AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON` is the runner-bound env var the k8s sandbox uses for server-side materialization. Schema (kebab-case id regex + 256 KiB cap) lives in `packages/sandbox-k8s/src/pod-spec.ts` and `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/installed-skills.ts` (mirror validators), not a central file.

## Test plan

- **Unit / pure-function:** parseSkillManifest (16) · stripCapabilitiesFromFrontmatter (6) · validateNewAttachments (8) · materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv (11).
- **Plugin + DB integration (testcontainers postgres):** SkillsStore CRUD (5) · skills plugin 5 service hooks (8) · skills_v1_skills migration (4) · agents migration with skill_attachments column · agents:any-attached-to-skill (3).
- **Admin HTTP routes:** /admin/skills CRUD (19 cases, all spec cases hit) · PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments (9 cases with slot-collision/binding-missing/binding-orphan).
- **Orchestrator integration:** 9 new cases in orchestrator.test.ts (union, three terminations, deleted-skill drop, no-skills:resolve fallback).
- **Sandbox materialization:** subprocess (5 new cases) · k8s pod-spec (4 new cases including 256 KiB cap) · runner main pre-SDK call (1 regression + 11 unit).
- **Component tests:** SkillsTab (7) · SkillEditor (8) · SkillAttachmentsSection (5).
- **Preset wiring:** k8s preset.test.ts expected-set assertion includes `@ax/skills`; both acceptance.test.ts files updated.
- **Workspace-wide:** `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` all green (0 errors, 2 pre-existing unrelated warnings).

## Follow-ups (deferred — not in scope)

- **System-prompt fold.** Phase 1 ships SDK-only — the SDK already indexes skill `description` into the system prompt at startup and the model invokes the built-in `Skill` tool to load bodies on demand. If a future MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk shows the model failing to invoke a skill it would have used with body-in-context, fold becomes a follow-up.
- **MCP-skill bundling (`capabilities.mcpServers`).** Reserved key rejects with `capability-deferred`. Bundling an MCP server inside a skill manifest is a later milestone.
- **Versioning / upgrade flow.** `version` is stored but only used at parse-time. A change-tracker + diff UI is deferred.
- **User-installable skills.** Phase 1 is admin-only (matching the design doc). Per-user skills would need a `/settings/skills*` route tree + scope on `skills:list/get`.
- **Workspace → installed promote flow.** Agent writes `.ax/skills/x/SKILL.md` (sans capabilities — stripped); an admin button promotes it into the installed table with chosen capability grants. Not yet built.
- **Automated e2e canary against a real postgres testcontainer + mocked GitHub server.** Out of scope here; the manual scenario in MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md is the e2e proof for now.
- **Pre-existing 2 lint warnings** in `deploy/charts/ax-next/__tests__/render.test.ts` and `packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/tool-use.test.tsx`. Untouched by this PR.

## Commits (21)

All 22 tasks complete. PR #96 is open: **https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/96**

## Summary

Implemented Phase 1 of the skill-install workflow across 21 commits:

- **`@ax/skills` plugin (new):** manifest parser with hardened capability schema, `skills_v1_skills` table, CRUD store, 5 service hooks (`list/get/upsert/delete/resolve`), `/admin/skills*` HTTP routes
- **`@ax/validator-skill` extension:** strip-and-warn `capabilities` blocks from workspace-authored SKILL.md
- **`@ax/agents` extension:** `skill_attachments` JSONB column, `agents:any-attached-to-skill` hook, `PATCH /admin/agents/:id/skill-attachments` with slot-collision validator
- **`@ax/chat-orchestrator` integration:** union skill `allowedHosts` + merge `credentialBindings` into `proxy:open-session`; three new termination outcomes (`skill-resolve-failed`, `skill-binding-missing`, `skill-slot-collision`)
- **Sandbox materialization:** `sandbox-subprocess` writes in-process; `sandbox-k8s` passes `AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON` env to the runner which materializes server-side before SDK spawn; both chmod 0555 after writes
- **Channel-web admin UI:** new Skills tab with list/create/edit/delete; SkillEditor with live-parsed preview; SkillAttachmentsSection embedded in AgentForm
- **Preset wiring:** `@ax/skills` loaded in k8s preset (CLI excluded — same sqlite/postgres-DDL pattern as `@ax/routines`)
- **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md:** new install→attach→chat scenario

**Verification:** `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` all green (0 lint errors; 2 pre-existing unrelated warnings). All 8 invariants (I-P1-1..8) have dedicated test coverage cited in the PR description.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.1.0/skills/receiving-code-review

# Code Review Reception

## Overview

Code review requires technical evaluation, not emotional performance.

**Core principle:** Verify before implementing. Ask before assuming. Technical correctness over social comfort.

## The Response Pattern

```
WHEN receiving code review feedback:

1. READ: Complete feedback without reacting
2. UNDERSTAND: Restate requirement in own words (or ask)
3. VERIFY: Check against codebase reality
4. EVALUATE: Technically sound for THIS codebase?
5. RESPOND: Technical acknowledgment or reasoned pushback
6. IMPLEMENT: One item at a time, test each
```

## Forbidden Responses

**NEVER:**
- "You're absolutely right!" (explicit CLAUDE.md violation)
- "Great point!" / "Excellent feedback!" (performative)
- "Let me implement that now" (before verification)

**INSTEAD:**
- Restate the technical requirement
- Ask clarifying questions
- Push back with technical reasoning if wrong
- Just start working (actions > words)

## Handling Unclear Feedback

```
IF any item is unclear:
  STOP - do not implement anything yet
  ASK for clarification on unclear items

WHY: Items may be related. Partial understanding = wrong implementation.
```

**Example:**
```
your human partner: "Fix 1-6"
You understand 1,2,3,6. Unclear on 4,5.

❌ WRONG: Implement 1,2,3,6 now, ask about 4,5 later
✅ RIGHT: "I understand items 1,2,3,6. Need clarification on 4 and 5 before proceeding."
```

## Source-Specific Handling

### From your human partner
- **Trusted** - implement after understanding
- **Still ask** if scope unclear
- **No performative agreement**
- **Skip to action** or technical acknowledgment

### From External Reviewers
```
BEFORE implementing:
  1. Check: Technically correct for THIS codebase?
  2. Check: Breaks existing functionality?
  3. Check: Reason for current implementation?
  4. Check: Works on all platforms/versions?
  5. Check: Does reviewer understand full context?

IF suggestion seems wrong:
  Push back with technical reasoning

IF can't easily verify:
  Say so: "I can't verify this without [X]. Should I [investigate/ask/proceed]?"

IF conflicts with your human partner's prior decisions:
  Stop and discuss with your human partner first
```

**your human partner's rule:** "External feedback - be skeptical, but check carefully"

## YAGNI Check for "Professional" Features

```
IF reviewer suggests "implementing properly":
  grep codebase for actual usage

  IF unused: "This endpoint isn't called. Remove it (YAGNI)?"
  IF used: Then implement properly
```

**your human partner's rule:** "You and reviewer both report to me. If we don't need this feature, don't add it."

## Implementation Order

```
FOR multi-item feedback:
  1. Clarify anything unclear FIRST
  2. Then implement in this order:
     - Blocking issues (breaks, security)
     - Simple fixes (typos, imports)
     - Complex fixes (refactoring, logic)
  3. Test each fix individually
  4. Verify no regressions
```

## When To Push Back

Push back when:
- Suggestion breaks existing functionality
- Reviewer lacks full context
- Violates YAGNI (unused feature)
- Technically incorrect for this stack
- Legacy/compatibility reasons exist
- Conflicts with your human partner's architectural decisions

**How to push back:**
- Use technical reasoning, not defensiveness
- Ask specific questions
- Reference working tests/code
- Involve your human partner if architectural

**Signal if uncomfortable pushing back out loud:** "Strange things are afoot at the Circle K"

## Acknowledging Correct Feedback

When feedback IS correct:
```
✅ "Fixed. [Brief description of what changed]"
✅ "Good catch - [specific issue]. Fixed in [location]."
✅ [Just fix it and show in the code]

❌ "You're absolutely right!"
❌ "Great point!"
❌ "Thanks for catching that!"
❌ "Thanks for [anything]"
❌ ANY gratitude expression
```

**Why no thanks:** Actions speak. Just fix it. The code itself shows you heard the feedback.

**If you catch yourself about to write "Thanks":** DELETE IT. State the fix instead.

## Gracefully Correcting Your Pushback

If you pushed back and were wrong:
```
✅ "You were right - I checked [X] and it does [Y]. Implementing now."
✅ "Verified this and you're correct. My initial understanding was wrong because [reason]. Fixing."

❌ Long apology
❌ Defending why you pushed back
❌ Over-explaining
```

State the correction factually and move on.

## Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| Performative agreement | State requirement or just act |
| Blind implementation | Verify against codebase first |
| Batch without testing | One at a time, test each |
| Assuming reviewer is right | Check if breaks things |
| Avoiding pushback | Technical correctness > comfort |
| Partial implementation | Clarify all items first |
| Can't verify, proceed anyway | State limitation, ask for direction |

## Real Examples

**Performative Agreement (Bad):**
```
Reviewer: "Remove legacy code"
❌ "You're absolutely right! Let me remove that..."
```

**Technical Verification (Good):**
```
Reviewer: "Remove legacy code"
✅ "Checking... build target is 10.15+, this API needs 13+. Need legacy for backward compat. Current impl has wrong bundle ID - fix it or drop pre-13 support?"
```

**YAGNI (Good):**
```
Reviewer: "Implement proper metrics tracking with database, date filters, CSV export"
✅ "Grepped codebase - nothing calls this endpoint. Remove it (YAGNI)? Or is there usage I'm missing?"
```

**Unclear Item (Good):**
```
your human partner: "Fix items 1-6"
You understand 1,2,3,6. Unclear on 4,5.
✅ "Understand 1,2,3,6. Need clarification on 4 and 5 before implementing."
```

## GitHub Thread Replies

When replying to inline review comments on GitHub, reply in the comment thread (`gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments/{id}/replies`), not as a top-level PR comment.

## The Bottom Line

**External feedback = suggestions to evaluate, not orders to follow.**

Verify. Question. Then implement.

No performative agreement. Technical rigor always.
Let me triage each comment against the code, then implement the valid ones.

+// Defensive fallback for parse-failure: log loud (stderr) but DO NOT throw.
+// A corrupt row would otherwise crash skills:list and freeze the admin UI;
+// the design doc explicitly tolerates this state (a `skills:resolve` of a
+// silently-malformed row drops it from the union, matching the "deleted-
+// skill-still-attached" policy). The loud log lets the operator find and
+// fix the row; the empty caps keep the rest of the system live.
+    // Use stderr directly — the store doesn't have a logger handle and
+    // pulling one in would couple the read path to AgentContext just
+    // for this defensive branch. The structured "msg" prefix matches
+    // the rest of the codebase's plain-text log convention.

git add packages/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(skills): address CodeRabbit review feedback

Addresses 12 of 14 PR #96 review comments. One rejected with reasoning
below; one outside-diff comment fixed alongside.

Bugfixes
--------

- **runner installed-skills empty-array handling.** AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON='[]'
  used to throw ENOENT on the final chmod (the loop never created the
  skills dir). Now short-circuits on empty array — matches the Phase 0
  default (init container left dir at 0o755; no work to lock). Adds a
  regression test for the empty-array bootstrap path.

- **sandbox-k8s session rollback on buildPodSpec throw.** buildPodSpec
  can now throw (e.g., 256 KiB AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON cap). The session
  was minted before that call, so a throw orphaned a live token with no
  pod. Wrap in try/catch + best-effort session:terminate, mirroring the
  existing createNamespacedPod failure handler.

- **AgentForm onSaved stale closure.** Replaces direct setEditing(...) in
  the SkillAttachmentsSection callback with a functional updater + identity
  guard so a slow save can't reopen a stale agent form after the user
  navigates away.

- **SkillEditor loading reset on skillId change.** Without resetting,
  switching from editing skill A to editing skill B briefly let A's
  bytes get saved to B before the new fetch resolved.

- **SkillsTab stuck-on-Loading bug.** When listSkills() rejected, the
  Alert rendered AND "Loading…" stayed (since skills === null was the
  loading signal). Now setSkills([]) on error so the error path renders
  the empty list alongside the alert.

Hardening
---------

- **agents store.ts skill_attachments validation.** Adds isStringRecord
  helper rejecting arrays + non-string + empty-string binding values
  before casting to SkillAttachment[]. Previously a malformed
  credentialBindings could surface as unvalidated state in the orchestrator.

- **skills manifest.ts wildcard pre-check.** Now catches block-list YAML
  forms (`allowedHosts:\n  - *.example.com`) in addition to the flow
  sequence form, so the pre-parse rejection surfaces invalid-host
  consistently regardless of how the manifest is written.

- **skills manifest.ts non-string slot description.** Was silently
  dropped, now rejects with invalid-slot — surfaces user errors that
  would otherwise pass validation by quiet shape coercion.

- **skills admin-routes splitSkillMd CRLF support.** Frontmatter fence
  regex now accepts both LF and CRLF — Windows-authored SKILL.md no
  longer 400s with "missing frontmatter fence".

- **skills admin-routes PUT manifest-name extraction.** The brittle
  `/^name:\s*(\S+)/` regex would reject valid quoted forms
  (`name: "github"`) with a false 400. Now accepts unquoted, single-
  quoted, and double-quoted scalars with optional trailing comments —
  same surface as the YAML spec.

- **skills store.ts corrupt-manifest logging.** Still defensively falls
  back to empty capabilities (so a corrupt row doesn't crash skills:list
  + freeze the admin UI — design doc tolerates this), but now logs loud
  to stderr with the skillId + error code so an operator can find and
  fix the row.

Tests
-----

- **store.test.ts updatedAt stricter assertion.** `new Date(garbage)`
  returns Invalid Date without throwing; the original test was non-
  effective. Now checks NaN + round-trip toISOString equality.

Rejected
--------

- **CodeRabbit #4 (channel-web/package.json:30): remove @ax/skills dep,
  move shared types to a neutral package.** Rejected. The @ax/skills/manifest
  subpath export is the agreed pattern (parallel to @ax/validator-routine:
  a pure-function frontmatter parser consumed by both the owning plugin
  and a sibling). Creating a new shared package for a single browser-side
  consumer of a single parser is over-engineering — the inline
  eslint-disable in SkillEditor.tsx documents the boundary explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push 2>&1 | tail -5
